title: [Bug] create-issues.yml regex skips wrong number of columns — issue URLs never populate INDEX.md
body:
**traceability_id:** `2026-04-16-ssas-ci-issue-regex-bug`

**Problem:** The Python regex in `.github/workflows/create-issues.yml` (line 89) uses `{4}` to skip columns after `traceability_id`, but the INDEX.md table has 8 columns. After `traceability_id`, 4 skips land on `created_at` — not the `issue` column. The regex needs `{5}` to reach the `issue` column containing `—`.

**Impact:** Issue URLs are never written to `docs/specs/INDEX.md`, silently breaking traceability.

**Proposed Solution:** Change `(?:[^|]*\|){4}` to `(?:[^|]*\|){5}` on line 89.

**Spec:** `docs/specs/2026/04/2026-04-16-ssas-ci-issue-regex-bug.md`

title: [Bug] create-prs.yml regex skips wrong number of columns — status never updates to in-review
body:
**traceability_id:** `2026-04-16-ssas-ci-pr-regex-bug`

**Problem:** The Python regex in `.github/workflows/create-prs.yml` (line 73) uses `{5}` to skip columns after `traceability_id`, but needs `{6}` to reach the `status` column. Currently it matches inside the `issue` column instead of `status`, so `open` to `in-review` updates never apply.

**Impact:** Spec status tracking is broken; PRs created by the workflow are invisible in INDEX.md lifecycle.

**Proposed Solution:** Change `(?:[^|]*\|){5}` to `(?:[^|]*\|){6}` on line 73.

**Spec:** `docs/specs/2026/04/2026-04-16-ssas-ci-pr-regex-bug.md`

title: [Docs] README version badge links to v1.0.0 — should be v3.10.0
body:
**traceability_id:** `2026-04-16-ssas-readme-version-badge`

**Problem:** README.md line 4 has a badge linking to `releases/tag/v1.0.0` with text `v2.0-improvements`. The actual version is `v3.10.0` per `package.json`.

**Impact:** Users see a misleading version, understating the project's maturity.

**Proposed Solution:** Update badge href to `/releases` and text to `v3.10.0-latest`.

**Spec:** `docs/specs/2026/04/2026-04-16-ssas-readme-version-badge.md`

title: [Docs] CLAUDE.md says '50+ vulnerability types' — actual count is 100+
body:
**traceability_id:** `2026-04-16-ssas-claude-md-vuln-count`

**Problem:** CLAUDE.md line 16 says `50+ vulnerability types` but README.md and the actual file contain 100+ (25 sections, 138+ subsections).

**Impact:** AI agents underestimate vulnerability-taxonomy.md scope.

**Proposed Solution:** Update CLAUDE.md line 16 from `50+` to `100+`.

**Spec:** `docs/specs/2026/04/2026-04-16-ssas-claude-md-vuln-count.md`

title: [Design] INDEX-vulns.md at 150-line limit — no room for new entries
body:
**traceability_id:** `2026-04-16-ssas-index-vulns-capacity`

**Problem:** `references/INDEX-vulns.md` is exactly 150 lines, hitting the maintenance ceiling defined in CLAUDE.md. Planned features (fusaka-eof, rwa-protocols, new severity trees) will need new index entries.

**Impact:** Blocks addition of new vulnerability categories to the index.

**Proposed Solution:** Consolidate related entries to create ~20-line buffer, or split into core/emerging sub-indexes.

**Spec:** `docs/specs/2026/04/2026-04-16-ssas-index-vulns-capacity.md`

title: [Feature] Add Ethena / Synthetic Stablecoins DeFi coverage
body:
**traceability_id:** `2026-04-16-ssas-ethena-synthetic-stablecoins`

**Problem:** Synthetic stablecoin protocols (Ethena USDe, delta-neutral designs) are a major 2024-2025 DeFi primitive with unique attack surfaces not covered by existing checklists. The skill covers restaking and perpetual DEXs separately but misses the combined delta-neutral pattern.

**Impact:** Auditors may miss synthetic stablecoin-specific vulnerabilities (funding rate manipulation, insurance fund solvency, custodian risk).

**Proposed Solution:** Add a 'Synthetic Stablecoins / Delta-Neutral' section to `defi-checklist.md` and USDe integration patterns to `defi-integrations.md`.

**Spec:** `docs/specs/2026/04/2026-04-16-ssas-ethena-synthetic-stablecoins.md`

title: [Improvement] Add .npmignore to exclude internal files from published package
body:
**traceability_id:** `2026-04-16-ssas-npmignore-publish`

**Problem:** No `.npmignore` exists. `npm pack` includes `.github/`, `docs/`, `.claude/`, and other internal files in the published tarball.

**Impact:** Published package is larger than necessary and exposes internal project management artifacts.

**Proposed Solution:** Create `.npmignore` excluding `.github/`, `.claude/`, `docs/`, `CLAUDE.md`, and editor configs.

**Spec:** `docs/specs/2026/04/2026-04-16-ssas-npmignore-publish.md`
